source: User-pasted Google Maps listing + pasted dinner/lunch menu

listing.name: Azul Mexicano
listing.address: 1078c E Montague Ave, North Charleston, SC 29405

sections: Appetizers (nachos variants, queso fundido, ceviche, dips, fried calamari, wings, chicken fingers, sopes)
Soups & Salads (tortilla soup, shrimp/seafood soup, taco salad, Azul special salad, Mil Amores salad, Azul chicken salad, Cancun salad)
Tortas (Mexican sub sandwiches with fries or street corn)
Tacos (street, fish - grilled or breaded, shrimp, gringo, golden fried, nopales, alambre)
Sizzling Fajitas (chicken, steak, shrimp, pork, combinations)
Enchiladas (multiple sauces: mole, ranchero, green, poblano)
Azul Specialties (molcajete, fried chimichanga, chilaquiles, taquitos mexicanos, milanesa, chicken/azul bowls)
From the Grill - Chicken/Beef/Seafood/Pork (served with choice of two sides)
For Vegetarians (veggie fajitas, veggie quesadilla, tofu tacos al pastor, portobello tacos, veggie soup)
Sides (guacamole, avocado, beans, rice, cheese, sour cream, pico de gallo, street corn, chips & salsa)
Dessert (flan, sopapilla, churros with ice cream, fried ice cream, tres leches cake)

Lunchtime Specials (11am-3pm M-F): breakfast burrito, carne asada, carnitas, fajitas, pollo ranchero, chori-pollo, chilaquiles, huevos al gusto, taco salad, chimichangas, azul steak burrito, fajita quesadilla, azul chicken salad, cancun salad, chile colorado, chile verde, quesadilla rellena, muy rapido lunch

analysisNotes: No allergen disclaimer, allergen guide, or menu labeling was present anywhere in the pasted menu — unlike some other restaurants added to this list, there's no explicit 'tell your server about allergies' language. Ask staff directly.
Fish tacos explicitly offer a choice of grilled OR breaded fish, and many tacos/sopes use corn tortillas by default — good naturally gluten-free options if ordered carefully.
A dedicated 'For Vegetarians' section (veggie fajitas, veggie quesadilla, tofu tacos al pastor, portobello tacos, veggie soup) shows built-in substitution flexibility beyond simple meat swaps.
Several fried items (chimichangas, taquitos, churros, sopapilla) mix corn- and flour-based fried components, likely from shared fryers — ask about cross-contact for gluten-free orders.
Cheese sauces, mole, and other prepared sauces appear across many dishes with no ingredient breakdown given — ask about thickeners/roux if avoiding gluten.
